text:
Once upon a time, in a quiet town filled with fields of sunflowers, there was a girl named Lily. She loved reading about adventures in faraway lands, pouring over books about pirates and knights. Every weekend, she’d find a quiet corner in the town’s library and spend hours diving into stories.

One sunny afternoon, instead of reading, Lily decided to have an adventure right there in the library. She imagined herself sailing to an island filled with talking animals and buried treasure.

She found a dusty ancient-looking book about sea voyages, slipped a leaf into it, and waved goodbye. That evening, her dreams were filled with mermaids and treasure chests brimming with golden jewels.

Week after week, Lily went back to that corner of the library, clutching her book close to her heart. Then one day, a familiar voice called out. It was Mr. Jenkins, the old librarian, who loved nothing more than hearing about Lily’s imagined adventures.

“You’re always here,” he said
